body_txt: A character is noticeably larger than one or more of their parents. Often Played for Laughs , but can sometimes be Played for Drama . Either way, it's often used for irony since we expect younger characters to be smaller than older characters. Can be justified if the child is the result of Interspecies Adoption or if their parents just have a significant size difference themselves. Despite the name, the character doesn't literally have to be a child. This is especially common if the character is an adult and the parents are Miniature Senior Citizens . If the character was born that large, then it can lead to Fridge Horror as to how the small biological mother gave birth to them. Can overlap with Tiny Guy, Huge Girl for father-daughter pairings, and Huge Guy, Tiny Girl for mother-son pairings. The kid's parents could also be one of these pairings. See Big Little Brother for a sibling variation. Contrast Pint-Sized Kid , Short Teens, Tall Adults , Gigantic Adults, Tiny Babies . This is a lot more common in real life than you'd expect for various reasons—younger generations having access to better nutrition than their parents, a recessive tall gene in the family, etc. Thus, listing real life examples would be pointless . As many children reach their adult height as teens, it's not uncommon to see a mother with a teenage son who's One Head Taller than her.
